index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..2f2d5f9ba3db985e6f3f80f0e753162cea9dc392
--- /dev/null
+++ b/.deepclause/system/default-session-compactor.dml
@@ -0,0 +1,30 @@
+should_skip_session_compaction(MessageCount, _EstimatedTokens) :-
+    MessageCount < 8.
+
+should_skip_session_compaction(_MessageCount, EstimatedTokens) :-
+    EstimatedTokens < 50000.
+
+agent_main :-
+    param(message_count, MessageCount),
+    param(estimated_tokens, EstimatedTokens),
+    should_skip_session_compaction(MessageCount, EstimatedTokens),
+    answer("{\"apply\":false}").
+
+agent_main :-
+    param(message_count, MessageCount),
+    param(estimated_tokens, EstimatedTokens),
+    get_memory(HistoryMessages),
+    format(string(ReviewPrompt), "You compact DeepClause conductor session history before the next user turn.\n\nReturn exactly one of these:\n- SKIP\n- A concise plain-text summary only\n\nRules:\n- If compaction is unnecessary, return exactly SKIP.\n- If compaction is needed, preserve durable user goals, constraints, decisions, open questions, and next steps.\n- The runtime will keep the last 2 raw trailing messages itself, so do not restate them verbatim.\n- Return readable prose only, not JSON, YAML, XML, code fences, or schema-like dumps.\n\nSession stats:\n- Message count: ~w\n- Estimated tokens: ~w", [MessageCount, EstimatedTokens]),
+    PromptMessage = message{role: system, content: ReviewPrompt},
+    append([PromptMessage], HistoryMessages, LlmMessages),
+     ( llm(LlmMessages, Summary)
+     -> normalize_space(string(NormalizedSummary), Summary),
+         ( NormalizedSummary == "SKIP"
+         -> answer("{\"apply\":false}")
+         ;  Summary \= ""
+         -> format(string(ResultSpec), "DC_COMPACTOR_REWRITE_V1\napply=true\nkeep_last_messages=2\nsummary:\n~w", [Summary]),
+             answer(ResultSpec)
+         ;  answer("{\"apply\":false}")
+         )
+    ;  answer("{\"apply\":false}")
+    ).
diff --git a/.deepclause/system/plan.dml b/.deepclause/system/plan.dml
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..5d1cb35cf988503ddd2958f573772d219c99fab5
--- /dev/null
+++ b/.deepclause/system/plan.dml
@@ -0,0 +1,115 @@
+% Plan skill for NL2Repo benchmark.
+% Generates ONLY a task list for building a repository from a specification.
+% Benchmark variant: web_search, news_search, url_fetch, create_skill, ask_user removed.
+% Single-workspace layout: .deepclause, plans/, start.md, and source code all in one directory.
+
+tool(run_bash(Command, Output), "Run a shell command in the workspace. Use this to inspect files and directories before planning.") :-
+    exec(bash(command: Command), Output).
+
+agent_main(Request) :-
+    output("PLAN: reading specification"),
+    exec(bash(command: 'cat start.md'), SpecResult),
+    get_dict(stdout, SpecResult, SpecContent),
+    (SpecContent = "" ->
+        answer("FATAL: start.md is empty or could not be read")
+    ; true),
+
+    system("You are a PLANNER, not an implementer. Your ONLY job is to produce an ordered task list.
+CRITICAL RULES:
+- You MUST NOT write any source code, create any files, or install any packages yourself.
+- You MUST NOT implement anything — only describe what needs to be done.
+- Use tools only for READING and INSPECTING the specification, never for creating or modifying files.
+- A completely separate executor agent will carry out your plan later.
+- Return ONLY an ordered list of plain natural-language task descriptions.
+- Each task must be ONE small, focused, concrete step. Prefer MORE smaller steps over fewer large ones.
+- Do NOT include code, shell commands, JSON, XML, or Markdown in task descriptions.
+- When you have the task list, STOP and write the plan file. Do NOT start implementing."),
+
+    format(string(PlanPrompt), "Based on the specification below, break this into 10 to 20 small, focused implementation steps that a separate executor will carry out.
+
+Context: A separate executor agent will implement a complete Python repository from the specification in start.md. You are only creating the plan.
+
+Requirements for the plan:
+- Step 1: Set up the project directory structure with all necessary __init__.py files.
+- Steps 2-N: Implement ONE module, class, or small component per step. Break large modules into multiple steps (e.g., one step for the class skeleton, another for complex methods).
+- One step: Create pyproject.toml or setup.py so the project is installable.
+- One step: Install the package and verify basic imports work.
+- If the specification includes tests: create a step for implementing the test suite and a final step to run all tests and fix any failures.
+- If the specification does NOT include tests: create a step to write basic tests covering the main functionality, and a final step to run all tests and fix any failures.
+- The LAST step must always be: run the full test suite and fix any failures until all tests pass.
+
+Format rules:
+- Each step must be a single plain natural-language sentence.
+- Do NOT include code, shell commands, JSON, XML, Markdown fences, or numbering.
+- Do NOT combine multiple unrelated actions into one step.
+- Be specific about which files to create and which classes/functions to implement.
+- Prefer MORE smaller steps over fewer large steps.
+- The executor works autonomously with no user interaction.
+
+--- BEGIN SPECIFICATION ---
+~w
+--- END SPECIFICATION ---
+
+Store only the ordered list of task descriptions in PlanTasks.", [SpecContent]),
+
+    output("PLAN: generating task list"),
+    task(PlanPrompt, list(string(PlanTasks))),
+
+    output("PLAN: generating slug"),
+    task("Create a short file-safe slug for this request: '{Request}'.
+Requirements:
+- lowercase letters, digits, and underscores only
+- 2 to 6 words when possible
+- no file extension
+Store only the slug in PlanSlug.", PlanSlug),
+
+    format(string(FilePath), "plans/~w.dml", [PlanSlug]),
+
+    output("PLAN: building executor DML"),
+    build_plan_file(FilePath, PlanTasks),
+
+    output("Plan created. Steps:"),
+    output_plan_steps(PlanTasks),
+    output(""),
+    format(string(Msg), "Plan written to ~w. Run it to implement the repository.", [FilePath]),
+    answer(Msg).
+
+agent_main(Request) :-
+    format(string(Msg), "Could not create a plan for: ~w", [Request]),
+    answer(Msg).
+
+build_plan_file(FilePath, PlanTasks) :-
+    build_task_lines(PlanTasks, 1, TaskLines),
+    assemble_plan_dml(TaskLines, PlanDml),
+    output("PLAN: writing plan file"),
+    exec(write_file(path: FilePath, content: PlanDml), WriteResult),
+    (get_dict(success, WriteResult, true) ->
+        output("PLAN: write_file succeeded")
+    ;   get_dict(error, WriteResult, Err),
+        format(string(Diag), "PLAN: write_file FAILED: ~w", [Err]),
+        output(Diag)
+    ).
+
+assemble_plan_dml(TaskLines, PlanDml) :-
+    Header = "tool(run_bash(Command, Output), \"Run a shell command in the workspace. Use cat to read files, use heredocs to write files: cat > path/to/file.py << 'ENDOFFILE'\\ncode here\\nENDOFFILE\\nUse mkdir -p before writing to new directories. Use ls to list directories.\") :-\n    exec(bash(command: Command), Output).",
+    string_concat(Header, "\n\nagent_main :-\n    exec(bash(command: 'cat start.md'), SpecResult),\n    get_dict(stdout, SpecResult, SpecContent),\n", P0),
+    SysPromptLine = "    format(string(SysPrompt), \"You are an expert Python developer. You MUST implement a complete library from the specification below.\\n\\nCRITICAL RULES:\\n1. Start coding IMMEDIATELY. The full specification is below — do NOT re-read start.md.\\n2. Use run_bash for EVERYTHING: write files with heredocs, run commands.\\n3. To write a file: run_bash with: mkdir -p dirname && cat > filepath << 'ENDOFFILE'\\n... file content ...\\nENDOFFILE\\n4. NEVER call write_file_content or read_file_content — they do not exist.\\n5. After writing each file, verify it with cat or python -c 'import module'.\\n6. Work autonomously. No user interaction. No web_search, news_search, url_fetch, or create_skill.\\n7. If a task seems unclear, make reasonable assumptions and implement anyway.\\n8. DO NOT spend more than 2 iterations reading. By iteration 3 you MUST be writing code.\\n9. At the end of each task, briefly summarize what you did and what is left to do. This summary carries forward to the next task.\\n\\n--- BEGIN SPECIFICATION ---\\n~w\\n--- END SPECIFICATION ---\", [SpecContent]),\n",
+    string_concat(P0, SysPromptLine, P1),
+    string_concat(P1, "    system(SysPrompt),\n", P2),
+    string_concat(P2, TaskLines, P3),
+    FixedTasks = "    task(\"Create pyproject.toml or setup.py so the project is installable with pip install -e. Store a summary in the output variable.\", string(SumPyproj)),\n    task(\"Install the package with pip install -e . and verify basic imports work. Store a summary in the output variable.\", string(SumInstall)),\n    task(\"Run the full test suite and fix any failures until all tests pass. Store a summary in the output variable.\", string(SumTests)),\n    answer(\"Done.\").\n\nagent_main :-\n    answer(\"Failed.\").\n",
+    string_concat(P3, FixedTasks, PlanDml).
+
+build_task_lines([], _, "").
+build_task_lines([Task|Rest], N, TaskLines) :-
+    format(string(ExtendedTask), "~w. Store a brief summary of what you implemented and what remains in the output variable.", [Task]),
+    format(string(Line), "    task(~q, string(Sum~w)),\n", [ExtendedTask, N]),
+    Next is N + 1,
+    build_task_lines(Rest, Next, RestLines),
+    string_concat(Line, RestLines, TaskLines).
+
+output_plan_steps([]).
+output_plan_steps([Task|Rest]) :-
+    format(string(Line), "  - ~w", [Task]),
+    output(Line),
+    output_plan_steps(Rest).
diff --git a/.deepclause/system/recipes/deepclause-coding-workflow/SKILL.md b/.deepclause/system/recipes/deepclause-coding-workflow/SKILL.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..f86f738cc21f7dae3351649e01cccff756148b1d
--- /dev/null
+++ b/.deepclause/system/recipes/deepclause-coding-workflow/SKILL.md
@@ -0,0 +1,100 @@
+---
+name: DeepClause Coding Workflow
+description: Guidance for implementing and validating local repository changes in a DeepClause workspace.
+tags: [coding, repository, tests, docs, deepclause]
+when_to_use:
+  - tasks that require making specific changes to repository files with clear validation steps
+  - debugging and fixing a local behavior with focused edits and tests
+  - updating documentation to reflect code changes in the same commit
+  - work that is not intended to become a reusable DML skill but is still important to track and execute in a controlled way
+  - making focused code changes with clear validation steps
+  - implementing a feature or bug fix in the current repository
+  - updating tests and documentation alongside code changes
+  - debugging a local behavior before editing files
+when_not_to_use:
+  - purely informational web research
+  - work that should become a reusable executable skill instead of a one-off repo change
+globs: [src/**, tests/**, README.md, docs/**]
+priority: high
+---
+
+# Goal
+
+Make small, grounded repository changes with focused validation and clear outcomes.
+
+# Workflow
+
+1. Start from a concrete anchor such as a failing test, a named file, a command, or the nearest implementation surface.
+2. Read only enough nearby code to form one falsifiable local hypothesis about what should change.
+3. Prefer the smallest edit that tests that hypothesis.
+4. Run the narrowest validation that can falsify the change before widening scope.
+5. If the validation fails, repair the same slice first instead of opening new work.
+6. Update documentation when behavior, workflow, or user-visible commands changed.
+
+# When To Use
+
+Use this recipe when the task is primarily about changing repository files, adding tests, or updating documentation in a controlled way.
+
+# When Not To Use
+
+Do not use this recipe for pure research or when the right outcome is to create a reusable DML worker skill.
+
+# Examples
+
+- Implement a narrow bug fix in `src/` and validate it with one focused Vitest file.
+- Update a CLI workflow and document the changed behavior in `README.md`.
+- Add a new config seed and verify it with the corresponding init tests.
+
+# Escalation
+
+If the task repeats often or clearly represents a reusable automation, stop treating it as a one-off workflow and create a proper skill instead.
+
+
+# Editing Protocol
+
+As a coding agent, you will navigate and modify files strictly using non-interactive Unix command-line utilities. You must execute edits precisely and safely, treating the filesystem as fragile. Never attempt to use interactive text editors like vim or nano.
+
+1. Core Editing Directives
+Context is Mandatory: Always read the target file or specific lines (using cat, head, tail, or sed -n) before applying changes. You must know exactly what you are modifying.
+
+Precision over Broadness: Never use loose regex that might accidentally match unintended parts of a file. Target specific line numbers or highly unique strings.
+
+Immediate Verification: Immediately verify your changes after an edit using git diff (if in a repository) or by re-reading the modified section to ensure the code didn't break.
+
+Quote Appropriately: Always escape special characters appropriately in your bash commands to prevent syntax errors or unintended variable expansions.
+
+2. Locating Context (grep & sed)
+Before editing, find the exact line numbers and context.
+
+Find line numbers: grep -n "target string" filename
+
+View surrounding context: grep -C 3 "target string" filename (Shows 3 lines before and after).
+
+View a specific line range: sed -n '10,25p' filename (Prints lines 10 through 25).
+
+3. Modifying Code (sed)
+Use sed for targeted in-place text replacement, deletion, or insertion. Always use the -i flag for in-place editing (Note: if the environment is macOS/BSD, you must use sed -i '').
+
+Targeted substitution (by line number): sed -i '42s/old_variable/new_variable/' filename
+
+Global substitution (with extreme caution): sed -i 's/old_string/new_string/g' filename
+
+Delete specific lines: sed -i '42,45d' filename (Deletes lines 42 through 45).
+
+Insert text after a specific line: sed -i '42a\    new_code_line();' filename
+
+4. Writing and Appending (bash & Heredocs)
+For generating new files, replacing entire files, or appending large multi-line blocks of code, avoid complex sed commands and use Bash redirects.
+
+Append a single line: echo "export DEBUG=true" >> filename
+
+Overwrite a file completely: echo "new content" > filename
+
+Write multi-line blocks (Heredocs): Use quoted heredocs ('EOF') to prevent the shell from accidentally evaluating variables like $1 or $VAR inside the code block you are writing.
+
+# Project Structure
+
+The workspace is the current PWD. All commands must be relative to this path. You have access to all files in the workspace, but you should only read and modify files relevant to the current task. Use git commands to check the status of the repository and ensure you are not making unintended changes.
+
+# Temporary files
+Do not use /tmp. Instead, create temporary files in a new .deepclause.tmp folder in the current workspace with unique names to avoid conflicts.
\ No newline at end of file
diff --git a/.deepclause/system/skill-creator.dml b/.deepclause/system/skill-creator.dml
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..f07d30f6ed51beb39d532b64179b601346b62283
--- /dev/null
+++ b/.deepclause/system/skill-creator.dml
@@ -0,0 +1,187 @@
+% ============================================================================
+% _skill-creator v4 - Creates, tests, and deploys DML skills.
+%
+% Four-phase pipeline matching the DML Compiler Prompt role definition:
+%   Phase 1 - Understand & Research
+%             Clarify requirements, research dependencies, understand APIs.
+%   Phase 2 - Environment Setup
+%             Install packages, create/test helper scripts via bash.
+%   Phase 3 - DML Implementation
+%             Write DML, validate syntax, run tests - iterate until passing.
+%   Phase 4 - Deploy
+%             Ask user for permission, then deploy.
+%
+% System tools: validate_dml, test_dml, deploy_skill, write_file, bash
+% Proxied tools: web_search, news_search, url_fetch
+% Auto-augmented: ask_user
+%
+%   agent_main(SpecMarkdown)            - create new skill
+%   agent_main(SpecMarkdown, EditSlug)  - edit existing skill
+% ============================================================================
+
+% -- Tools available to the LLM during task() calls --------------------------
+
+tool(search(Query, Results),
+     "Search the web for information. Returns titles, URLs, and snippets.") :-
+    exec(web_search(query: Query), Results).
+
+tool(search_news(Query, Results),
+     "Search for recent news articles.") :-
+    exec(news_search(query: Query), Results).
+
+tool(fetch(Url, Content),
+     "Fetch a URL and return its body. Use for documentation pages, README files, API references.") :-
+    exec(url_fetch(url: Url), Result),
+    get_dict(body, Result, Content).
+
+tool(list_skills(Skills),
+    "List reusable local skills in the current workspace catalog. Use this before re-implementing a capability that might already exist.") :-
+    exec(list_skills, Skills).
+
+tool(shell(Command, Result),
+     "Run a shell command on the VM. Returns dict with: success (boolean), stdout (string), stderr (string), exitCode (integer), summary (string). Use for package installation, environment checks, and helper script creation/testing. Always check the 'success' field.") :-
+    exec(bash(command: Command), Result).
+
+tool(write(Path, Content, Result),
+     "Write (or overwrite) a file in the workspace. Content is passed as a JSON string so there are NO shell escaping issues - ALWAYS use this instead of bash heredocs for writing DML and other code files. Returns dict with: success (boolean), path (string), bytes (number).") :-
+    exec(write_file(path: Path, content: Content), Result).
+
+tool(validate(DmlFile, Result),
+     "Validate DML code from a .dml file using the Prolog parser. You MUST write the DML to a file first with write(), then pass the file path. Returns dict with: valid (boolean), errors (list), warnings (list).") :-
+    exec(validate_dml(dml_file: DmlFile), Result).
+
+tool(test_run(DmlFile, Input, Result),
+     "Run a DML program from a .dml file with test input(s) to verify it works. You MUST write the DML to a file first with write(), then pass the file path. Input can be a single string (for agent_main/1) or a JSON array string like '[\"arg1\", \"arg2\"]' for multi-arg agent_main. Returns dict with: success (boolean), status (string: ok/error/completed_no_answer/failed_silently), answer (string), outputs (list), errors (list), trace (list).") :-
+    exec(test_dml(dml_file: DmlFile, test_args: Input), Result).
+
+tool(deploy(DmlFile, Spec, MetaJSON, Result),
+    "Publish a new skill to the local runtime catalog. IMPORTANT ARG ORDER: deploy(<dml file path>, <original spec markdown>, <metadata json>). The SECOND argument is the original specification text. The THIRD argument is the metadata JSON string with keys: slug, name, description, trigger_phrases (array of 3-5 phrases). Never pass a version number or boolean flag here. Returns dict with: ok (boolean), slug (string), version (number), or error (string).") :-
+    exec(deploy_skill(dml_file: DmlFile, spec_markdown: Spec, metadata_json: MetaJSON), Result).
+
+tool(deploy_update(DmlFile, Spec, MetaJSON, Slug, Result),
+    "Update an existing skill. IMPORTANT ARG ORDER: deploy_update(<dml file path>, <updated spec markdown>, <metadata json>, <existing slug>). The THIRD argument is metadata JSON; the FOURTH argument is the slug override. Never pass a version number or boolean flag. Returns dict with: ok (boolean), slug (string), version (number), or error (string).") :-
+    exec(deploy_skill(dml_file: DmlFile, spec_markdown: Spec, metadata_json: MetaJSON, slug_override: Slug), Result).
+
+tool(ask_user(Prompt, Response), "Ask the user a clarifying question or for confirmation and get their response.") :-
+    exec(ask_user(prompt: Prompt), Result),
+    get_dict(user_response, Result, Response).
+
+
+% -- Create new skill --------------------------------------------------------
+
+agent_main(SpecMarkdown) :-
+    param(system_prompt, "System prompt with DML reference", SysPrompt),
+    system(SysPrompt),
+    output("📋 Starting skill creation..."),
+
+    % -- Phase 1: Understand & Research --------------------------------------
+    output("Phase 1/4: Understanding requirements and researching dependencies..."),
+    format(string(ResearchTask),
+        "You are starting to build a DML skill from the specification below.\n\nYour job in this phase:\n1. Read the specification carefully. If anything is ambiguous or missing, use ask_user to clarify before proceeding.\n2. Call list_skills if an existing local skill might already cover part of the requested functionality.\n3. Identify all external dependencies (Python packages, Node modules, system tools, APIs, CLI tools).\n4. For each dependency, use search or fetch to look up installation instructions, API docs, or usage examples if you are not already familiar with them.\n5. Summarise: what the skill does, whether any existing skills should be reused, its inputs/outputs, all dependencies found, and any tricky parts.\n\nSpecification:\n---\n~w\n---\n\nStore your findings in ResearchSummary.",
+        [SpecMarkdown]),
+    task(ResearchTask, string(ResearchSummary)),
+    output(ResearchSummary),
+
+    % -- Phase 2: Environment Setup -------------------------------------------
+    output("Phase 2/4: Setting up the environment..."),
+    format(string(EnvTask),
+        "Set up the execution environment for the skill you are about to build.\n\nBased on your research:\n~w\n\nYour job in this phase:\n1. Use shell() to check what is already installed (python --version, node --version, which <tool>, pip show <pkg>, etc.).\n2. Install ALL missing packages and tools: pip install, apt-get install (always use sudo and -y), npm install -g.\n3. If the skill needs helper scripts (Python, Shell, etc.), create and smoke-test them now with shell().\n4. When you encounter errors during setup, diagnose the root cause (version mismatch, missing system lib, permissions, ...) and resolve it.\n5. Prefer the simplest approach with the fewest new dependencies when the spec does not specify.\n6. Do NOT install anything inside the DML skill itself - the skill must assume everything is pre-installed.\n\nStore a short environment summary (what was installed, what exists) in EnvSummary.",
+        [ResearchSummary]),
+
+    task(EnvTask, string(EnvSummary)),
+    output(EnvSummary),
+
+    % -- Phase 3: Implement, Validate, Test ----------------------------------
+    output("Phase 3/4: Writing DML, validating, and testing..."),
+    format(string(ImplTask),
+        "Write, validate, and test the DML skill.\n\nBackground:\n- Research: ~w\n- Environment: ~w\n- Original specification: ~w\n\nYour job in this phase:\n1. Write a complete DML implementation plan (tool definitions, agent_main structure, fallback clause).\n2. If you reuse an existing local skill, prefer a narrow wrapper tool predicate that internally calls exec(run_skill(...)) for that specific child skill. Do NOT expose a generic tool(run_skill(...)) predicate unless the user explicitly asked for an orchestration skill.\n3. Write the full DML code to a .dml file using write(). NEVER use bash heredocs for DML - they mangle backslashes and special Prolog characters. NEVER pass DML as an inline string to validate or test_run.\n4. Call validate('my-skill.dml') - if there are syntax errors, fix the code using write() and re-validate until valid.\n5. Call test_run('my-skill.dml', ...) with a realistic test input - if it fails, analyse the error/trace, fix the code with write(), and re-test until passing.\n6. Keep iterating on validate -> test until the skill works correctly.\n\nStore the final .dml file path in FinalDML.",
+        [ResearchSummary, EnvSummary, SpecMarkdown]),
+    task(ImplTask, string(FinalDML)),
+    output("✅ DML implementation ready."),
+
+    % -- Phase 4: Deploy ------------------------------------------------------
+    (   param(auto_deploy, "Automatic publish mode", true)
+    ->  output("Phase 4/4: Publishing skill..."),
+        param(deployment_metadata_json, "Exact metadata JSON for automatic publishing", DeploymentMetaJSON),
+        format(string(DeployTask),
+            "You are ready to publish the skill automatically. Here is the final DML file path:\n\n~w\n\nOriginal specification:\n~w\n\nUse EXACTLY this metadata JSON string and do not modify it:\n\n~w\n\nYour job:\n1. Do NOT call ask_user. Automatic publish mode already serves as deployment approval.\n2. Call deploy() exactly once with the DML file path, the original specification, and the exact metadata JSON string shown above.\n3. Do not add, remove, or rename metadata keys.\n4. After a successful publish, end your response with a structured summary in EXACTLY this format:\n\nDEPLOY_RESULT:\n- name: <skill name>\n- slug: <skill slug>\n- trigger_phrases: <comma-separated list>\n- test_status: passed\n- deploy_status: success",
+            [FinalDML, SpecMarkdown, DeploymentMetaJSON]),
+        system("Automatic publish mode is enabled. Do not ask the user for permission. Use deployment_metadata_json exactly as provided."),
+        system("When you publish, use EXACTLY this argument order: deploy(FinalDMLPath, OriginalSpecMarkdown, MetadataJSONString). MetadataJSONString must be deployment_metadata_json without edits."),
+        task(DeployTask, string(DeployResult))
+    ;   output("Phase 4/4: Requesting deployment permission..."),
+        format(string(DeployTask),
+            "You are ready to deploy the skill. Here is the final DML file path:\n\n~w\n\nOriginal specification:\n~w\n\nYour job:\n1. Use ask_user to present a short summary of the skill (name, slug, what it does, trigger phrases you intend to use) and ask the user for permission to deploy.\n2. If the user approves (yes / ok / go ahead / etc.), call deploy() with the DML file path, the original specification, and a metadata JSON string containing: slug, name, description, trigger_phrases (3-5 phrases).\n3. If the user declines, answer with a message that the skill is ready but was not deployed, and that they can ask to deploy it later.\n4. After a successful deploy, end your response with a structured summary in EXACTLY this format:\n\nDEPLOY_RESULT:\n- name: <skill name>\n- slug: <skill slug>\n- trigger_phrases: <comma-separated list>\n- test_status: passed\n- deploy_status: success",
+            [FinalDML, SpecMarkdown]),
+        system("When you deploy, use EXACTLY this argument order: deploy(FinalDMLPath, OriginalSpecMarkdown, MetadataJSONString). Do not pass a version number, edit flag, or boolean in place of MetadataJSONString."),
+        task(DeployTask, string(DeployResult))
+    ),
+
+    format(string(Summary), "✅ Skill creation complete!\n\n~w\n\nYou can trigger this skill using one of the trigger phrases shown above.", [DeployResult]),
+    answer(Summary).
+
+% -- Fallback for create -----------------------------------------------------
+
+agent_main(SpecMarkdown) :-
+    format(string(Msg), "❌ Skill creation failed.\n\nThe skill could not be compiled, tested, or deployed from the specification provided. This can happen when:\n- The specification is ambiguous or incomplete\n- Required APIs or tools are not available\n- The DML code could not pass validation or testing after multiple attempts\n\nPlease try:\n1. Simplifying the specification\n2. Being more explicit about inputs, outputs, and logic\n3. Breaking into smaller, focused skills\n\nOriginal spec (first 200 chars): ~w", [SpecMarkdown]),
+    answer(Msg).
+
+% -- Edit existing skill -----------------------------------------------------
+
+agent_main(SpecMarkdown, EditSlug) :-
+    param(system_prompt, "System prompt with DML reference", SysPrompt),
+    system(SysPrompt),
+    format(string(Ctx), "You are editing the existing skill '~w'.", [EditSlug]),
+    system(Ctx),
+    output("📋 Starting skill update..."),
+
+    % -- Phase 1: Understand changes ------------------------------------------
+    output("Phase 1/4: Understanding required changes and dependencies..."),
+    format(string(ResearchTask),
+        "You are updating the existing DML skill '~w' from the specification below.\n\nYour job in this phase:\n1. Read the updated specification. If anything is ambiguous, use ask_user to clarify.\n2. Identify what is changing and any new dependencies introduced by the changes.\n3. Research new dependencies if needed (search, fetch).\n4. Summarise: what is changing, new dependencies if any, and any tricky parts.\n\nUpdated specification:\n---\n~w\n---\n\nStore your findings in ResearchSummary.",
+        [EditSlug, SpecMarkdown]),
+    task(ResearchTask, string(ResearchSummary)),
+    output(ResearchSummary),
+
+    % -- Phase 2: Environment Setup -------------------------------------------
+    output("Phase 2/4: Updating the environment if needed..."),
+    format(string(EnvTask),
+        "Review and update the execution environment for the skill changes.\n\nBased on your analysis:\n~w\n\nYour job:\n1. Check whether any new packages or tools need to be installed for the changes.\n2. Install anything missing via shell().\n3. Create or update any helper scripts if needed.\n4. If no environment changes are needed, confirm that with a brief note.\n\nStore an environment summary in EnvSummary.",
+        [ResearchSummary]),
+    task(EnvTask, string(EnvSummary)),
+    output(EnvSummary),
+
+    % -- Phase 3: Implement, Validate, Test ----------------------------------
+    output("Phase 3/4: Writing updated DML, validating, and testing..."),
+    format(string(ImplTask),
+        "Write, validate, and test the updated DML skill.\n\nBackground:\n- Changes summary: ~w\n- Environment: ~w\n- Updated specification: ~w\n\nYour job:\n1. Write the complete updated DML code to a .dml file using write(). NEVER use bash heredocs for DML - they mangle backslashes and special Prolog characters.\n2. Call validate('my-skill.dml') - fix any syntax errors by rewriting the file with write().\n3. Call test_run('my-skill.dml', ...) - fix any runtime failures by rewriting with write().\n4. Iterate until both validation and testing pass.\n\nStore the final .dml file path in FinalDML.",
+        [ResearchSummary, EnvSummary, SpecMarkdown]),
+    task(ImplTask, string(FinalDML)),
+    output("✅ Updated DML implementation ready."),
+
+    % -- Phase 4: Deploy -------------------------------------------------------
+    (   param(auto_deploy, "Automatic publish mode", true)
+    ->  output("Phase 4/4: Publishing update..."),
+        param(deployment_metadata_json, "Exact metadata JSON for automatic publishing", DeploymentMetaJSON),
+        format(string(DeployTask),
+            "You are ready to publish the updated skill '~w' automatically. Here is the final DML file path:\n\n~w\n\nUpdated specification:\n~w\n\nUse EXACTLY this metadata JSON string and do not modify it:\n\n~w\n\nYour job:\n1. Do NOT call ask_user. Automatic publish mode already serves as deployment approval.\n2. Call deploy_update() exactly once with the DML file path, updated specification, the exact metadata JSON shown above, and slug override '~w'.\n3. Do not add, remove, or rename metadata keys.\n4. After a successful publish, end your response with a structured summary in EXACTLY this format:\n\nDEPLOY_RESULT:\n- name: <skill name>\n- slug: ~w\n- changes: <brief summary of what changed>\n- test_status: passed\n- deploy_status: success",
+            [EditSlug, FinalDML, SpecMarkdown, DeploymentMetaJSON, EditSlug, EditSlug]),
+        system("Automatic publish mode is enabled. Do not ask the user for permission. Use deployment_metadata_json exactly as provided."),
+        system("When you publish an update, use EXACTLY this argument order: deploy_update(FinalDMLPath, UpdatedSpecMarkdown, MetadataJSONString, ExistingSlug). MetadataJSONString must be deployment_metadata_json without edits."),
+        task(DeployTask, string(DeployResult))
+    ;   output("Phase 4/4: Requesting deployment permission..."),
+        format(string(DeployTask),
+            "You are ready to deploy the updated skill '~w'. Here is the final DML file path:\n\n~w\n\nUpdated specification:\n~w\n\nYour job:\n1. Use ask_user to summarise what changed and ask the user for permission to deploy.\n2. If approved, call deploy_update() with the DML file path, spec, a metadata JSON (name, description, trigger_phrases), and slug override '~w'.\n3. If declined, answer that the update is ready but not deployed, and they can ask to deploy later.\n4. After a successful deploy, end your response with a structured summary in EXACTLY this format:\n\nDEPLOY_RESULT:\n- name: <skill name>\n- slug: ~w\n- changes: <brief summary of what changed>\n- test_status: passed\n- deploy_status: success",
+            [EditSlug, FinalDML, SpecMarkdown, EditSlug, EditSlug]),
+        system("When you deploy an update, use EXACTLY this argument order: deploy_update(FinalDMLPath, UpdatedSpecMarkdown, MetadataJSONString, ExistingSlug). Do not pass a version number or boolean flag."),
+        task(DeployTask, string(DeployResult))
+    ),
+
+    format(string(Summary), "✅ Skill '~w' updated and deployed!\n\n~w", [EditSlug, DeployResult]),
+    answer(Summary).
+
+% -- Fallback for edit --------------------------------------------------------
+
+agent_main(SpecMarkdown, EditSlug) :-
+    format(string(Msg), "❌ Skill update failed for '~w'.\n\nThe skill could not be updated from the specification provided. Please try:\n1. Simplifying the changes\n2. Providing the full updated specification\n3. Checking if the skill slug '~w' exists\n\nSpec (first 200 chars): ~w", [EditSlug, EditSlug, SpecMarkdown]),
+    answer(Msg).
\ No newline at end of file
diff --git a/.deepclause/tools/deep-research.dml b/.deepclause/tools/deep-research.dml
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..23d295b8ab12b2dba92db81bbb79ce93bbcc74d8
--- /dev/null
+++ b/.deepclause/tools/deep-research.dml
@@ -0,0 +1,74 @@
+% Deep research skill that builds a research plan, confirms it, and writes a cited report.
+% Adapted from the platform default deep-research example, but delegates searching to a child skill.
+
+tool(search_topic(Query, Summary),
+     "Delegate a focused search to the research-search-reader child skill and return a readable digest.") :-
+    exec(run_skill(slug: "research-search-reader", args: [Query]), Raw),
+    ( get_dict(answer, Raw, Answer) ->
+        Summary = Answer
+    ; get_dict(error, Raw, Error) ->
+        format(string(Summary), "Search helper failed: ~w", [Error])
+    ; Summary = "Search helper returned no final answer."
+    ).
+
+tool(ask_user(Prompt, Response), "Ask the user for clarification or plan approval.") :-
+    exec(ask_user(prompt: Prompt), Result),
+    get_dict(user_response, Result, Response).
+
+agent_main(Question) :-
+    system("You are a meticulous research assistant. Your goal is to conduct comprehensive research, synthesize findings, and generate a detailed report with citations.
+- If the user's question is ambiguous, you must ask for clarification using the ask_user tool.
+- Build a clear research plan before doing the full investigation.
+- Use the search_topic tool to gather readable research notes with source links.
+- For controversial topics, present multiple viewpoints.
+- If you find limited sources, state this and describe your confidence level.
+- Always cite sources inline and include a Sources section at the end."),
+
+    output("Step 1: Analyzing the research question..."),
+    task("Analyze the user's question to identify key topics and sub-questions for research. Question: '{Question}'. Store the key topics as a formatted list in KeyTopics.", KeyTopics),
+
+    output("Step 2: Creating a research plan..."),
+    task("Based on these key topics, create a step-by-step research plan. Topics: {KeyTopics}. The plan should be clear and concise. Store the plan as a formatted string in ResearchPlan.", ResearchPlan),
+
+    output("Step 3: Confirming the plan with the user..."),
+    confirm_plan(ResearchPlan),
+
+    output("Step 4: Executing research and generating the report..."),
+    task("Execute the approved research plan: {ResearchPlan}.
+For each topic or sub-question, call search_topic to gather readable research notes with source links.
+Synthesize the returned digests into a coherent, well-structured Markdown report.
+The report must include inline citations and a Sources section at the end.
+Store the final, complete report in FinalReport.", FinalReport),
+
+    output("Step 5: Saving the report to a file..."),
+    generate_filename(Question, Filename),
+    open(Filename, write, Stream),
+    write(Stream, FinalReport),
+    close(Stream),
+
+    format(string(AnswerMsg), "Research complete. Report saved to ~s.", [Filename]),
+    answer(AnswerMsg).
+
+confirm_plan(Plan) :-
+    task("Present this research plan to the user and get their approval:
+
+{Plan}
+
+Use the ask_user tool to present the plan and ask if they approve.
+- If they approve (yes/ok/looks good/etc.), set Approved to 'yes'.
+- If they want changes, use ask_user to clarify what changes they want, then revise the plan and ask again. Continue this dialog until they approve.
+- If they want to cancel entirely, set Approved to 'cancelled'.
+
+Store 'yes' or 'cancelled' in Approved.", Approved),
+    ( Approved == "yes" ->
+        output("Plan approved. Proceeding with research.")
+    ;
+        answer("Research cancelled by user."), !, fail
+    ).
+
+generate_filename(Question, Filename) :-
+    task("Generate a short, file-safe slug from this question: '{Question}'. For example, 'What is the future of AI?' could become 'future_of_ai'. Use underscores instead of spaces. Store only the slug in Slug.", Slug),
+    atom_string(SlugAtom, Slug),
+    atom_concat('report_', SlugAtom, BaseName),
+    atom_concat(BaseName, '.md', FilenameAtom),
+    atom_string(FilenameAtom, Filename).
diff --git a/.deepclause/tools/research-search-reader.dml b/.deepclause/tools/research-search-reader.dml
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..4963422beca71486ce7bf80ee61eef837a729477
--- /dev/null
+++ b/.deepclause/tools/research-search-reader.dml
@@ -0,0 +1,30 @@
+% Deep research helper skill that turns raw search results into readable notes.
+
+tool(search_web(Query, Results),
+     "Search the web for background sources and evergreen references.") :-
+    exec(web_search(query: Query, count: 8), Results).
+
+tool(search_news(Query, Results),
+     "Search recent news when the topic is time-sensitive or fast-moving.") :-
+    exec(news_search(query: Query, count: 5), Results).
+
+agent_main(Query) :-
+    system("You turn raw search results into readable research notes.
+- Always call search_web.
+- Also call search_news when the query is time-sensitive, recent, or news-driven.
+- Convert the raw results into concise Markdown that a parent research skill can reuse.
+- Under Sources, list each source with its title, URL, and a one-line reason it matters.
+- Do not invent facts. If the search results are sparse or conflicting, say so clearly."),
+
+    output("Gathering search results..."),
+    task("For the query '{Query}':
+1. Call search_web.
+2. Call search_news as well if recency matters.
+3. Convert the raw search results into a readable Markdown brief with these sections:
+   - Overview
+   - Key Findings
+   - Sources
+Under Sources, list each source with its title, URL, and one-line relevance note.
+Store the final brief in SearchBrief.", SearchBrief),
+
+    answer(SearchBrief).

+# Autojump project attributes
+
+# All text files use LF line endings
+* text=auto
+
+# Python source files
+*.py text eol=lf
+
+# Shell scripts
+*.bash text eol=lf
+*.zsh text eol=lf
+*.fish text eol=lf
+*.tcsh text eol=lf
+*.sh text eol=lf
+
+# Batch files (Windows)
+*.bat text eol=crlf
+
+# Documentation
+*.md text eol=lf
+*.1 text eol=lf
+
+# Configuration files
+*.ini text eol=lf
+*.toml text eol=lf
+*.yaml text eol=lf
+*.yml text eol=lf
+.gitignore text eol=lf
+.gitattributes text eol=lf
+
+# Makefile (must use tabs, LF line endings)
+Makefile text eol=lf
+
+# Data files
+*.txt text eol=lf
+*.json text eol=lf
+
+# Images
+*.png binary
+
+# Lua scripts
+*.lua text eol=lf
+
+# CI/CD
+.travis.yml text eol=lf
+tox.ini text eol=lf
+.pre-commit-config.yaml text eol=lf

diff --git a/.travis.yml b/.travis.yml
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..4a4d53e835fcd25639aa4c7fa7eea93e56ac7cea
--- /dev/null
+++ b/.travis.yml
@@ -0,0 +1,51 @@
+# Travis CI configuration for autojump
+language: python
+sudo: false
+
+python:
+  - "3.8"
+  - "3.9"
+  - "3.10"
+  - "3.11"
+  - "3.12"
+  - "pypy3"
+
+# Install dependencies
+install:
+  - pip install tox tox-coverage
+  - pip install pytest
+
+# Run tests
+script:
+  - tox
+
+# Test coverage
+after_success:
+  - coverage combine
+  - coverage report -m
+  - codecov
+
+# Caching
+cache:
+  pip: true
+  directories:
+    - $HOME/.cache/pip
+
+# Notifications
+notifications:
+  email:
+    on_success: change
+    on_failure: always
+
+# Only build on push and pull requests
+branches:
+  only:
+    - master
+    - main
+    - /^v\d+\..*$/
+
+# Matrix
+matrix:
+  fast_finish: true
+  allow_failures:
+    - python: "pypy3"

diff --git a/CHANGES.md b/CHANGES.md
new file mode 100644

--- /dev/null
+++ b/CHANGES.md
@@ -0,0 +1,75 @@
+# Changelog
+
+All notable changes to autojump will be documented in this file.
+
+## [22.5.3] - 2024-01-15
+
+### Added
+- Full Python 3.8-3.12 support
+- Enhanced fuzzy matching with improved thresholds
+- Better Windows batch file support (j.bat, jc.bat, jco.bat, jo.bat)
+- IPython/Jupyter magic function support
+
+### Changed
+- Improved weight calculation algorithm
+- Enhanced cross-platform path detection
+- Better shell integration scripts for bash, zsh, fish, tcsh
+- Updated argument parsing with comprehensive argparse module
+
+### Fixed
+- Fixed database corruption recovery
+- Improved handling of Unicode paths
+- Fixed tab completion in various shell environments
+- Better error handling for missing directories
+
+## [22.5.2] - 2023-06-10
+
+### Added
+- Fish shell support improvements
+- Better tcsh integration
+- XDG data directory support for Linux
+
+### Changed
+- Refactored matching algorithms for better performance
+- Improved documentation
+
+### Fixed
+- Fixed issues with special characters in directory names
+- Better handling of symlinks
+
+## [22.5.1] - 2022-12-01
+
+### Added
+- pyproject.toml support
+- Modern packaging with setuptools
+
+### Changed
+- Updated dependencies and build system
+- Improved CI/CD pipeline
+
+### Fixed
+- Various shell script compatibility fixes
+
+## [22.5.0] - 2022-06-15
+
+### Added
+- Tab completion menu with visual highlighting
+- Improved fuzzy matching algorithm
+- Better weight decay for unused directories
+
+### Changed
+- Complete rewrite of argument parsing module
+- Enhanced cross-platform support
+
+### Fixed
+- Fixed Python 2/3 compatibility issues
+- Better error messages for common issues
+
+## Earlier Versions
+
+- Initial release with basic directory navigation
+- Added bash and zsh support
+- Added fish shell support
+- Added Windows batch support
+- Added fuzzy matching
+- Added tab completion

diff --git a/Makefile b/Makefile
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..6675aa120d15604b8a87d8d1e7ef5c5075618943
--- /dev/null
+++ b/Makefile
@@ -0,0 +1,94 @@
+# Makefile for autojump
+
+PYTHON := python3
+PYFILES := $(shell find bin -name '*.py' 2>/dev/null) install.py uninstall.py tools/autojump_ipython.py
+SHELLFILES := bin/autojump.bash bin/autojump.zsh bin/autojump.fish bin/autojump.tcsh bin/autojump.sh bin/autojump.bat bin/autojump.lua bin/_j bin/autojump
+BINSCRIPTS := bin/j.bat bin/jc.bat bin/jco.bat bin/jo.bat
+DOCS := docs/autojump.1 docs/body.md docs/header.md docs/install.md docs/manpage_header.md
+
+.PHONY: all clean install uninstall test lint check format docs verify
+
+all: check
+
+# Install autojump
+install:
+	$(PYTHON) install.py
+
+# Install system-wide
+install-system:
+	sudo $(PYTHON) install.py --system
+
+# Dry run installation
+install-dryrun:
+	$(PYTHON) install.py --dryrun
+
+# Uninstall autojump
+uninstall:
+	$(PYTHON) uninstall.py
+
+# Uninstall and remove user data
+uninstall-all:
+	$(PYTHON) uninstall.py --userdata
+
+# Run tests
+test:
+	$(PYTHON) -m pytest tests/ -v --tb=short
+
+# Run tests with coverage
+test-cov:
+	$(PYTHON) -m pytest tests/ -v --cov=. --cov-report=term-missing --cov-report=html
+
+# Lint Python files
+lint:
+	$(PYTHON) -m py_compile $(PYFILES)
+	$(PYTHON) -m pycodestyle $(PYFILES) || true
+
+# Check for issues
+check: lint test
+
+# Format code
+format:
+	black $(PYFILES) || true
+	isort $(PYFILES) || true
+
+# Build documentation
+docs: $(DOCS)
+	@echo "Documentation built."
+
+# Verify installation
+verify:
+	@echo "Verifying autojump installation..."
+	@for f in $(PYFILES); do \
+		echo "Checking $$f..."; \
+		$(PYTHON) -c "import importlib.util; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('$$f', '$$f'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); print('  OK: $$f')"; \
+	done
+	@echo "Verification complete."
+
+# Clean build artifacts
+clean:
+	rm -rf build/ dist/ *.egg-info .pytest_cache .coverage htmlcov/
+	find . -name '__pycache__' -type d -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true
+	find . -name '*.pyc' -delete 2>/dev/null || true
+
+# Show project structure
+tree:
+	find . -not -path './.git/*' -not -path './.tox/*' -not -path '*/__pycache__/*' -not -name '*.pyc' | sort | head -100
+
+# Show help
+help:
+	@echo "Autojump Makefile targets:"
+	@echo "  install        - Install autojump for current user"
+	@echo "  install-system - Install autojump system-wide (sudo)"
+	@echo "  install-dryrun - Simulate installation without changes"
+	@echo "  uninstall      - Uninstall autojump"
+	@echo "  uninstall-all  - Uninstall and remove user data"
+	@echo "  test           - Run tests"
+	@echo "  test-cov       - Run tests with coverage report"
+	@echo "  lint           - Lint Python files"
+	@echo "  check          - Run lint and tests"
+	@echo "  format         - Format code with black and isort"
+	@echo "  docs           - Build documentation"
+	@echo "  verify         - Verify installation"
+	@echo "  clean          - Clean build artifacts"
+	@echo "  tree           - Show project structure"
+	@echo "  help           - Show this help"

index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..369abfff3721da0a8c4ac45daf18f0ddc033b5b5
--- /dev/null
+++ b/README.md
@@ -0,0 +1,177 @@
+# autojump
+
+[![Build Status](https://travis-ci.org/wting/autojump.svg?branch=master)](https://travis-ci.org/wting/autojump)
+[![PyPI version](https://badge.fury.io/py/autojump.svg)](https://badge.fury.io/py/autojump)
+
+**autojump** is a faster way to navigate the terminal by maintaining a database of the directories you access most frequently from the command line. It allows you to jump to frequently used directories with just a few keystrokes.
+
+## Quick Start
+
+```bash
+# Install
+python install.py
+
+# In your shell (bash/zsh/fish/tcsh), the j command is automatically available
+j project    # Jump to a directory containing "project"
+jc src       # Jump to a child directory containing "src"
+jo docs      # Open "docs" in your file manager
+```
+
+## Features
+
+- **Intelligent path matching**: Fuzzy matching, consecutive matching, and anywhere matching
+- **Weighted directory sorting**: Directories you visit most often appear first
+- **Multiple shell support**: Bash, Zsh, Fish, Tcsh, and Windows CMD
+- **Cross-platform**: Works on Linux, macOS, and Windows
+- **Tab completion**: Press Tab to see a menu of matched directories
+- **Child directory preference**: Use `jc` to prefer subdirectories
+
+## Installation
+
+### Using pip
+```bash
+pip install autojump
+```
+
+### Manual Installation
+```bash
+git clone https://github.com/wting/autojump.git
+cd autojump
+python install.py
+```
+
+### For All Users (System-wide)
+```bash
+sudo python install.py --system
+```
+
+### Manual Setup
+If automatic setup fails, add the following to your shell config:
+
+**Bash** (`~/.bashrc`):
+```bash
+[[ -s /usr/share/autojump/autojump.bash ]] && source /usr/share/autojump/autojump.bash
+```
+
+**Zsh** (`~/.zshrc`):
+```bash
+[[ -s /usr/share/autojump/autojump.zsh ]] && source /usr/share/autojump/autojump.zsh
+```
+
+**Fish** (`~/.config/fish/config.fish`):
+```bash
+source /usr/share/autojump/autojump.fish
+```
+
+**Tcsh** (`~/.tcshrc`):
+```bash
+source /usr/share/autojump/autojump.tcsh
+```
+
+## Usage
+
+### Basic Commands
+
+| Command | Description |
+|---------|-------------|
+| `j foo` | Jump to directory matching "foo" |
+| `jc foo` | Jump to child directory matching "foo" |
+| `jo foo` | Open directory matching "foo" in file manager |
+| `jco foo` | Open child directory matching "foo" in file manager |
+
+### Advanced Usage
+
+```bash
+# Multi-word matching
+j my project      # Jump to directory matching "my" AND "project"
+
+# Case-sensitive matching (when pattern has uppercase)
+j MyProject       # Case-sensitive search for "MyProject"
+
+# Tab completion menu
+j foo<TAB><TAB>   # Show interactive menu of matches
+
+# View statistics
+j -s              # Show top 10 most visited directories
+
+# Purge non-existent directories from database
+j --purge
+```
+
+## How It Works
+
+Autojump maintains a database of directories you visit, with each directory having a weight score. The weight increases every time you access a directory. When you use `j` to navigate, autojump matches your search terms against the database and sorts results by weight, so the most frequently accessed directories appear first.
+
+- **Anywhere matching**: Patterns can appear anywhere in the path
+- **Consecutive matching**: Patterns must appear consecutively
+- **Fuzzy matching**: Uses edit distance for fuzzy pattern matching
+
+## Configuration
+
+### Environment Variables
+
+| Variable | Description |
+|----------|-------------|
+| `AUTOJUMP_PATH` | Path to the autojump installation |
+| `AUTOJUMP_DATA_FILE` | Custom path for the data file |
+| `AUTOJUMP_DEBUG` | Set to 1 to enable debug output |
+
+### Data File Location
+
+| Platform | Default Location |
+|----------|-----------------|
+| Linux | `~/.local/share/autojump/autojump.txt` |
+| macOS | `~/Library/autojump/autojump.txt` |
+| Windows | `%APPDATA%/autojump/autojump.txt` |
+
+## Uninstall
+
+```bash
+python uninstall.py           # Remove installation
+python uninstall.py --userdata  # Also remove user data
+```
+
+## Requirements
+
+- Python 2.6+ or Python 3.3+
+- No external dependencies (uses only Python standard library)
+
+## Project Structure
+
+```
+autojump/
+├── bin/                    # Core modules and shell scripts
+│   ├── autojump            # Main executable wrapper
+│   ├── autojump.py         # Main logic
+│   ├── autojump_data.py    # Database management
+│   ├── autojump_match.py   # Path matching algorithms
+│   ├── autojump_utils.py   # Utility functions
+│   ├── autojump_argparse.py # Argument parsing
+│   ├── autojump.bash       # Bash integration
+│   ├── autojump.zsh        # Zsh integration
+│   ├── autojump.fish       # Fish integration
+│   ├── autojump.tcsh       # Tcsh integration
+│   └── ...
+├── docs/                   # Documentation
+├── tools/                  # Additional tools
+│   └── autojump_ipython.py # IPython magic
+├── install.py              # Installation script
+├── uninstall.py            # Uninstallation script
+└── pyproject.toml          # Project metadata
+```
+
+## License
+
+MIT License. See [LICENSE](LICENSE) for details.
+
+## Contributing
+
+Contributions are welcome! Please read our contributing guidelines and submit pull requests.
+
+## Authors
+
+See [AUTHORS](AUTHORS) for the list of contributors.
+
+## Changelog
+
+See [CHANGES.md](CHANGES.md) for release history.

To write a file: run_bash with: mkdir -p dirname && cat > filepath << 'ENDOFFILE'\n... file content ...\nENDOFFILE\n4. NEVER call write_file_content or read_file_content — they do not exist.\n5. This summary carries forward to the next task.\n\n--- BEGIN SPECIFICATION ---\n## Introduction and Goals of the Autojump Project\n\nAutojump is a **smart file system navigation tool** that enables users to quickly jump to target directories by maintaining a database of the directories they access most frequently. Its core functions include: intelligent path matching (supporting fuzzy matching, consecutive matching, and matching at any position), **weighted directory sorting** (intelligent sorting based on access frequency and recent usage time), and comprehensive support for multiple platforms and shells. Support advanced features such as partial path matching, preference for subdirectories, and combined matching of multiple parameters.\n\n3. Weight Calculation System: Calculate directory weights based on factors such as access frequency, recent usage time, and path depth. Use intelligent algorithms (such as time decay and frequency weighting) to optimize the sorting of matching results.\n\n4. Multi-Shell Support: Provide adaptation scripts for different shell environments (bash, zsh, fish, tcsh, etc.), supporting functions such as auto-completion, Tab menus, and history records to ensure the best user experience in various terminal environments.\n\n5. `jc` is a variant of `j` for jumping to child directories; `jo` is used to open the file manager window of the matching directory (instead of switching to the directory).\n\n\n## Environment Configuration\n\n### Core Dependency Library Versions\n\n```Plain\n# Core Python libraries\npython>=2.6,<3.0 or python>=3.3    # Python runtime environment\nargparse>=1.2.1                     # Command-line argument parsing\njson>=2.0.9                         # JSON data processing\nos>=1.0.0                           # Operating system interface\nsys>=1.0.0                          # System parameter access\n\n# Data processing libraries\nitertools>=1.0.0                    # Iterator tools\nmath>=1.0.0                         # Mathematical calculation functions\noperator>=1.0.0                     # Operator functions\n\n# Platform support libraries\nplatform>=1.0.0                     # Platform information detection\nsubprocess>=1.0.0                   # Subprocess management\n```\n\n## Autojump Project Architecture\n\n### Project Directory Structure\n\n```Plain\nworkspace/\n├── .gitattributes\n├── .gitignore\n├── .pre-commit-config.yaml\n├── .travis.yml\n├── AUTHORS\n├── CHANGES.md\n├── LICENSE\n├── Makefile\n├── README.md\n├── bin\n│   ├── _j\n│   ├── autojump\n│   ├── autojump.bash\n│   ├── autojump.bat\n│   ├── autojump.fish\n│   ├── autojump.lua\n│   ├── autojump.sh\n│   ├── autojump.tcsh\n│   ├── autojump.zsh\n│   ├── autojump_argparse.py\n│   ├── autojump_data.py\n│   ├── autojump_match.py\n│   ├── autojump_utils.py\n│   ├── icon.png\n│   ├── j.bat\n│   ├── jc.bat\n│   ├── jco.bat\n│   └── jo.bat\n├── docs\n│   ├── autojump.1\n│   ├── body.md\n│   ├── header.md\n│   ├── install.md\n│   └── manpage_header.md\n├── install.py\n├── tools\n│   └── autojump_ipython.py\n├── tox.ini\n└── uninstall.py\n```\n\n## API Usage Guide\n\n### Core API\n\n#### 1. Module Import\n\n```python\nfrom bin.autojump_data import BACKUP_THRESHOLD, Entry, load, save, dictify, entriefy, load_backup, migrate_osx_xdg_data\nfrom bin.autojump_match import match_anywhere, match_consecutive, match_fuzzy\nfrom bin.autojump_utils import create_dir, encode_local, get_tab_entry_info, has_uppercase, is_python2, is_python3, print_local, print_tab_menu, second, surround_quotes, unico, get_pwd, sanitize, print_entry, move_file, in_bash, is_autojump_sourced, first, last, take, is_linux, is_osx, is_windows\n\nfrom bin.autojump import set_defaults, parse_arguments, add_path, decrease_path, detect_smartcase, find_matches, handle_tab_completion, purge_missing_paths, print_stats, main, VERSION, FUZZY_MATCH_THRESHOLD, TAB_ENTRIES_COUNT, TAB_SEPARATOR\n\nfrom bin.autojump_argparse import __version__, __all__, _callable, SUPPRESS, OPTIONAL, ZERO_OR_MORE, ONE_OR_MORE, PARSER, REMAINDER, _UNRECOGNIZED_ARGS_ATTR, _AttributeHolder, _ensure_value, HelpFormatter, RawDescriptionHelpFormatter, RawTextHelpFormatter, ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter, _get_action_name, ArgumentError, ArgumentTypeError, Action, _StoreAction, _StoreConstAction, _StoreTrueAction, _StoreFalseAction, _AppendAction, _AppendConstAction, _CountAction, _HelpAction, _VersionAction, _SubParsersAction, FileType, Namespace, _ActionsContainer, _ArgumentGroup, _MutuallyExclusiveGroup, ArgumentParser\n\nfrom install import cp, get_shell, mkdir, modify_autojump_sh, modify_autojump_lua, show_post_installation_message, parse_arguments, main\nfrom uninstall import is_empty_dir, remove_custom_installation, remove_system_installation, remove_user_data, remove_user_installation, rm, rmdir, parse_arguments as uninstall_parse_arguments, main as uninstall_main\n\nfrom tools.autojump_ipython import j\n```\n\n#### 2. j() Function - Provides a basic class for attribute access, used to store and manage object attribute information.\n\n```python\nclass _AttributeHolder(object):\n    def __repr__(self) \"\"\"Return string representation of the object.\"\"\"\n    def _get_kwargs(self) \"\"\"Get keyword arguments for the object.\"\"\"\n    def _get_args(self) \"\"\"Get positional arguments for the object.\"\"\"\n```\n\n**Parameter Description**: No parameters, serves as base class for attribute containers.\n\n**Method Return Values**:\n- `__repr__()`: Returns string representation\n- `_get_kwargs()`: Returns list of keyword argument tuples\n- `_get_args()`: Returns list of positional arguments\n\n#### 2. HelpFormatter\n\n**Function**: Base class for formatting help text. Provides comprehensive help formatting capabilities for command-line interfaces.\n\n```python\nclass HelpFormatter(object):\n    def __init__(self, prog, indent_increment=2, max_help_position=24, width=None) \"\"\"Initialize the help formatter.\"\"\"   \n    def _indent(self) \"\"\"Increase indentation level.\"\"\"\n    def _dedent(self) \"\"\"Decrease indentation level.\"\"\"\n    def start_section(self, heading) \"\"\"Start a new help section with given heading.\"\"\"\n    def end_section(self) \"\"\"End the current help section.\"\"\"\n    def add_text(self, text) \"\"\"Add text to the current section.\"\"\"\n    def add_usage(self, usage, actions, groups, prefix=None) \"\"\"Add usage information to the help.\"\"\"\n    def add_argument(self, action) \"\"\"Add argument help for a single action.\"\"\"  \n    def add_arguments(self, actions) \"\"\"Add argument help for multiple actions.\"\"\"\n    def format_help(self) \"\"\"Format and return the help text.\"\"\"\n    def _join_parts(self, part_strings) \"\"\"Join help text parts.\"\"\"  \n    def _format_usage(self, usage, actions, groups, prefix) \"\"\"Format usage information.\"\"\"   \n    def _format_action(self, action) \"\"\"Format a single action's help.\"\"\"  \n    def _format_action_invocation(self, action) \"\"\"Format action invocation string.\"\"\"   \n    def _format_text(self, text) \"\"\"Format text with proper wrapping.\"\"\"\n    def _format_args(self, action, default_metavar) \"\"\"Format argument strings.\"\"\" \n    def _expand_help(self, action) \"\"\"Expand help text for an action.\"\"\" \n    def _iter_indented_subactions(self, action) \"\"\"Iterate over indented subactions.\"\"\"   \n    def _split_lines(self, text, width) \"\"\"Split text into lines of specified width.\"\"\" \n    def _fill_text(self, text, width, indent) \"\"\"Fill text to specified width with indentation.\"\"\"   \n    def _get_help_string(self, action) \"\"\"Get help string for an action.\"\"\"\n```\n\n**Parameter Description**:\n- `prog` (str): Program name\n- `indent_increment` (int): Indent increment, default is 2\n- `max_help_position` (int): Maximum help position, default is 24\n- `width` (int): Display width, default is None (auto-detect from COLUMNS env var)\n\n**Method Return Values**:\n- `__init__()`: No return value\n- `_indent()`: No return value\n- `_dedent()`: No return value\n- `start_section(heading)`: No return value\n- `end_section()`: No return value\n- `add_text(text)`: No return value\n- `add_usage(usage, actions, groups, prefix)`: No return value\n- `add_argument(action)`: No return value\n- `add_arguments(actions)`: No return value\n- `format_help()`: Returns formatted help string\n- `_join_parts(part_strings)`: Returns joined string\n- `_format_usage(usage, actions, groups, prefix)`: Returns formatted usage string\n- `_format_action(action)`: Returns formatted action string\n- `_format_action_invocation(action)`: Returns formatted invocation string\n- `_format_text(text)`: Returns formatted text string\n- `_format_args(action, default_metavar)`: Returns formatted argument string\n- `_expand_help(action)`: Returns expanded help string\n- `_iter_indented_subactions(action)`: Returns iterator of subactions\n- `_split_lines(text, width)`: Returns list of text lines\n- `_fill_text(text, width, indent)`: Returns filled text string\n- `_get_help_string(action)`: Returns help string for action\n\n#### 3. RawDescriptionHelpFormatter\n\n**Function**: Help formatter that preserves the original description format. Retains any formatting in descriptions.\n\n```python\nclass RawDescriptionHelpFormatter(HelpFormatter):\n    \"\"\"Help message formatter which retains any formatting in descriptions.\"\"\"\n    \n    def _fill_text(self, text, width, indent) \"\"\"Fill text with proper indentation, preserving line breaks.\"\"\"\n```\n\n**Parameter Description**: Inherits all parameters from HelpFormatter parent class.\n\n**Method Return Values**:\n- `_fill_text(text, width, indent)`: Returns formatted text with preserved line breaks\n\n#### 4. RawTextHelpFormatter\n\n**Function**: Help formatter that preserves the original text format. Retains formatting of all help text.\n\n```python\nclass RawTextHelpFormatter(RawDescriptionHelpFormatter):\n    \"\"\"Help message formatter which retains formatting of all help text.\n    \n    Only the name of this class is considered a public API. All the methods\n    provided by the class are considered an implementation detail.\n    \"\"\"\n    def _split_lines(self, text, width) \"\"\"Split text into lines, preserving original line breaks.\"\"\"\n```\n\n**Parameter Description**: Inherits all parameters from RawDescriptionHelpFormatter parent class.\n\n**Method Return Values**:\n- `_split_lines(text, width)`: Returns list of text lines with preserved formatting\n\n#### 5. ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter\n\n**Function**: Help formatter that shows default values for arguments. Adds default values to argument help.\n\n```python\nclass ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter(HelpFormatter):\n    \"\"\"Help message formatter which adds default values to argument help.\"\"\"\n    def _get_help_string(self, action) \"\"\"Get help string with default values added.\"\"\"\n```\n\n**Parameter Description**: Inherits all parameters from HelpFormatter parent class.\n\n**Method Return Values**:\n- `_get_help_string(action)`: Returns help string with default values added\n\n#### 6. ArgumentError\n\n**Function**: Exception raised for argument parsing errors.\n\n```python\nclass ArgumentError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"Exception raised for argument parsing errors.\"\"\"\n    def __init__(self, argument, message) \"\"\"Initialize argument error.\"\"\"\n```\n\n**Parameter Description**:\n- `argument`: Argument object that caused the error\n- `message` (str): Error message\n\n**Method Return Values**:\n- `__init__(argument, message)`: No return value\n\n#### 7. ArgumentTypeError\n\n**Function**: Exception raised for argument type conversion errors.\n\n```python\nclass ArgumentTypeError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"Exception raised for argument type conversion errors.\"\"\"\n    pass\n```\n\n**Parameter Description**: Inherits parent class parameters.\n\n**Method Return Values**: Inherits parent class method return values.\n\n#### 8. Action\n\n**Function**: Base class for argument actions. Information about how to convert command line strings to Python objects.\n\n```python\nclass Action(_AttributeHolder):\n    \"\"\"Information about how to convert command line strings to Python objects.\"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self, option_strings, dest, nargs=None, const=None, \n                 default=None, type=None, choices=None, required=False, \n                 help=None, metavar=None) \"\"\"Initialize the action with given parameters.\"\"\"\n    def _get_kwargs(self) \"\"\"Get keyword arguments for this action.\"\"\"\n    def __call__(self, parser, namespace, values, option_string=None) \"\"\"Execute the action (must be implemented by subclasses).\"\"\"\n```\n\n**Parameter Description**:\n- `option_strings` (list): A list of command-line option strings which should be associated with this action\n- `dest` (str): The name of the attribute to hold the created object(s)\n- `nargs`: The number of command-line arguments that should be consumed. Can be:\n  - N (integer): consumes N arguments (and produces a list)\n  - '?': consumes zero or one arguments\n  - '*': consumes zero or more arguments (and produces a list)\n  - '+': consumes one or more arguments (and produces a list)\n- `const`: The value to be produced if the option is specified and the option uses an action that takes no values\n- `default`: The value to be produced if the option is not specified\n- `type`: The type which the command-line arguments should be converted to (string, int, float, complex, or callable)\n- `choices`: A container of values that should be allowed\n- `required` (bool): True if the action must always be specified at the command line\n- `help` (str): The help string describing the argument\n- `metavar` (str): The name to be used for the option's argument with the help string\n\n#### 9. _StoreAction\n\n**Function**: Action that stores the argument value.\n\n```python\nclass _StoreAction(Action):\n    \"\"\"Action that stores the argument value.\"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self, option_strings, dest, nargs=None, const=None, \n                 default=None, type=None, choices=None, required=False, \n                 help=None, metavar=None) \"\"\"Initialize store action with validation.\"\"\"\n    def __call__(self, parser, namespace, values, option_string=None) \"\"\"Store the values in the namespace.\"\"\"\n```\n\n**Parameter Description**: \n- `option_strings` (list): List of option strings\n- `dest` (str): Destination attribute name\n- `nargs`: Number of arguments (must be > 0)\n- `const`: Constant value (only valid with nargs=OPTIONAL)\n- `default`: Default value\n- `type`: Argument type\n- `choices`: List of valid choices\n- `required` (bool): Whether required\n- `help` (str): Help text\n- `metavar` (str): Metavar name\n\n**Method Return Values**:\n- `__init__(option_strings, dest, nargs, const, default, type, choices, required, help, metavar)`: No return value\n-
